fullName: Distribution Connection and Use of System Agreement
issuer: DCUSA Ltd (the six DNO groups + IDNOs + suppliers)
authority: Multiparty industry agreement; compliance is a condition of the distribution and supply licences
summary: The commercial and charging framework for the distribution network — the distribution-level equivalent of CUSC. Sets out connection charging (fully shallow since April 2023), Distribution Use of System (DUoS) charging, the common connection charging methodology, and terms between DNOs, IDNOs, and suppliers.
scope: Schedule 18 — Common Distribution Charging Methodology (CDCM) for LV/HV DUoS
Schedule 22 — EHV Distribution Charging Methodology (EDCM) for EHV DUoS
Supplier/DNO commercial terms (metering, data exchange, credit)

keyParts.name: CCCM (Sch 2)
keyParts.detail: Since April 2023: fully shallow charging — the connecting customer pays only for sole-use extension assets; reinforcement is socialised via DUoS.

keyParts.name: CDCM (Sch 18)
keyParts.detail: Cost-reflective DUoS for sub-EHV connections (LV and HV).

keyParts.name: EDCM (Sch 22)
keyParts.detail: Forward-looking charges for EHV-connected demand and generation.

keyParts.name: Modification process
keyParts.detail: DCUSA Panel; Ofgem decides material modifications (DCP).
